title: Lactuca × favratii
taxonomy: Lactuca × favratii is a hybrid species within the Lactuca genus, which includes lettuce and related plants. It is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Within the Asteraceae family, Lactuca × favratii is placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae and tribe Lactuceae. The species is further classified as Lactuca × favratii Favrat, with Favrat being the authority who first described this hybrid. This classification system is based on the binomial nomenclature used in botany to categorize and identify plant species.
uses: Lactuca × favratii is a hybrid of Lactuca sativa and Lactuca serriola, but there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, its parent species are known to have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Lactuca sativa, for example, has been used to treat digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The leaves of Lactuca serriola have been used to treat fever and as a diuretic. More research would be needed to determine the specific medicinal uses of Lactuca × favratii.
benefits: Lactuca × favratii is a type of lettuce that is rich in nutrients and offers several health benefits. It is high in vitamins A and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, which can help support healthy digestion and boost the immune system. The antioxidants present in Lactuca × favratii may also help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Additionally, its high fiber content can promote satiety and support healthy blood sugar levels. Consuming Lactuca × favratii as part of a balanced diet may also contribute to overall cardiovascular health.
parts:
I couldn't find information on Lactuca × favratii, however Lactuca virosa (opium lettuce) which is closely related is used for its medicinal properties. 
Lactuca virosa contains lactucarium, a bitter and milky sap, which is used to treat various ailments. 
The sap can be used to treat insomnia, anxiety, and other nervous disorders, and is also used as a pain reliever. 
It is believed to have a sedative effect and is often used in traditional medicine. 
The sap is typically ingested in the form of tea or tincture.
preparations: Lactuca × favratii is a sterile hybrid lettuce, but information on its herbal preparations is limited and not widely documented. However, as a member of the Lactuca genus, Lactuca × favratii may share some properties with other lettuce species, which are sometimes used in herbal medicine. Some lettuce species are used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The leaves of Lactuca × favratii, like those of other lettuce species, contain lactucarium, a bitter compound that may have sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. More research is needed to determine the specific uses and benefits of Lactuca × favratii in herbal preparations.
intro: Lactuca × favratii, also known as Favrat's lettuce or prickly lettuce, is a hybrid species of the Lactuca genus, characterized by its edible leaves and taproot. The plant has been utilized for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lactuca × favratii include lactucarium, a sesquiterpene lactone responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as flavonoids and alkaloids. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat insomnia, anxiety, and other nervous system disorders.
